title: North and South
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
summary: When her father leaves the Church in a crisis of conscience, Margaret Hale is uprooted from her comfortable home in Hampshire to move with her family to the north of England.  Initially repulsed by the ugliness of her new surroundings in the industrial town of Milton, Margaret becomes aware of the poverty and suffering of the local mill workers and develops a passionate sense of social justice.

title: A Pair of Blue Eyes
author: Thomas Hardy
summary: Elfride Swancourt is the daughter of the Rector of Endelstow, a remote sea-swept parish in Corwall based on St Juliot, where Hardy began A Pair of Blue Eyes during the beginning of his courtship of his first wife, Emma. title: Beloved

summary: Toni Morrison--author of Song of Solomon and Tar Baby--is a writer of remarkable powers: her novels, brilliantly acclaimed for their passion, their dazzling language and their lyric and emotional force, combine the unassailable truths of experience and emotion with the vision of legend and imagination.  " Sethe lives in a small house on the edge of town with her daughter, Denver, her mother-in-law, Baby Suggs,...

title: The Complete Stories

He is most celebrated, however, for the casual notes and aphorisms that he collected in what he called his Waste Books.

author: Thomas Nashe

title: The Social Contract and Discourse on the Origin of Inequality

summary: This work presents two of Jean-Jacques Rousseau's most popular and influencial works The Social Contract and Discourse on the Origin and Basis of Inequality Among Men.  These works discuss a human state of nature and political community.

summary: One of the first books written on what is now called aesthetics.  , comedy), it has been very influential in western thought, such as the part on tragedy.

summary: 's The Signifying Monkey is a groundbreaking work that illuminates the relationship between the African and African-American vernacular traditions and black literature.  It elaborates a new critical approach located within this tradition that allows the black voice to speak for itself.

title: The Memory of Old Jack
author: Wendell Berry
summary: In a rural Kentucky river town, "Old Jack" Beechum, a retired farmer, sees his life again through the shades of one burnished day in September 1952.  Bringing the earthiness of America's past to mind, The Memory of Old Jack conveys the truth and integrity of the land and the people who live from it.

author: Susan Buck-Morss
summary: In this path-breaking work, Susan Buck-Morss draws new connections between history, inequality, social conflict, and human emancipation. summary: The biggest financial crisis since the Great Depression has generated a surge of interest in Marx's work in the effort to understand the origins of our current predicament. title: The Radicalism of the American Revolution

All the major European powers apart from Russia were heavily involved and, while each country started out with rational war aims, the fighting rapidly...

Aunada a la brutalidad propia de la vida militar, a lo largo de las páginas de esta extraordinaria novela, la vehemencia y...
